summary: This is a brief announcement post where Cathy O'Neil lists her upcoming speaking engagements in fall 2013. She will speak to Columbia's Data Science Society about data science thinking, participate in a panel on financial regulation transparency, give an MAA lecture on recommendation systems for high school math teachers, and present something 'nerdy and skeptical' at Harvard's Applied Statistics Workshop.
key_points: Speaking to engineers at Columbia about 'how to think like a data scientist'
Participating in financial reform panel discussion about what mathematical tools can and cannot do for regulation
Giving an accessible talk on recommendation systems ('Start Your Own Netflix') to high school math teachers
money_quotes: I'll be giving my two cents on what mathematical tools can do and cannot do with respect to this stuff.
I haven't figured out exactly what I'm talking about but it will be something nerdy and skeptical.
key_insight: O'Neil's speaking topics reveal her consistent focus on demystifying data science while maintaining healthy skepticism about mathematical tools' limitations.
